phase3_findings.description: DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md:378 mutates the implement-probe evidence-of-record head to a SHA that names no object, desyncing a three-site coupled mirror
budget doc maintainer note states a 3,538-word root ceiling that exists nowhere; the shipped ceiling is 3,567
cutover item 3's body says the first ceiling move landed at 3,567, contradicting its own 3,500->3,515 heading and item 5's second move
RAF_ROOT_CEIL comment claims 'four correctness fixes' while the shipped record documents five, omitting the one 3,567 is attributed to
changeset and lib/intervention-surfaces.md assert 'every fix loop' / 'every autonomous entry', contradicted by the same diff's Skill-denied-fallback carve-out
install.md promises an undeliverable extension 'halts every autonomous fix loop at skill entry', but the skill mandates only 'surface its stderr, never proceed silently'
the supersession guard's most-recent-edit and empty-or-page-full arms — its two most recently repaired fail-opens — carried no pin
RAF_PIN_NONBINDING pinned a phrase carrying none of the rule's operative content
the loader's two failure arms are discriminated only by exit status, which the prose never tells the run to read
the supersession guard's 'data to surface' arm names no schema field or report section to surface into
the second loader fence says 'failure arms as above' rather than restating them, so a trim of the first fence silently weakens the second

fix_decisions.claim_text: the loader's absent-vs-undeliverable arms are discriminated only by exit status, which the prose never instructs the run to read
fix_decisions.evidence: the shape is pre-existing on the first (review-and-fix) load fence, unchanged by this diff — git blame shows that sentence predates this PR; closing it means changing the skill's documented failure semantics for BOTH fences, beyond issue #620's port-and-load scope, and the root has ~4 words of headroom (3,563 of 3,567) so the added prose would force a fourth ceiling renegotiation

parking_evidence.basis: the defect is in pre-existing prose this PR does not modify; the diff only replicates the established arm by reference
parking_evidence.failing_input: an undeliverable receiving-code-review.md whose non-zero exit the run does not read, taking the silent no-op arm
parking_evidence.source: skills/review-and-fix/SKILL.md line 27 (pre-existing, outside this PR's diff hunks)

fix_decisions.claim_text: the supersession guard's 'data to surface' arm names no durable schema field or report section
fix_decisions.evidence: real gap, but binding it requires new top-level schema fields in the root record shape plus fixing.md item 7 and a loop-exit render — a design addition beyond this PR's scope, and unaffordable against the root's ~4-word headroom without a fourth ceiling renegotiation this iteration

fix_decisions.claim_text: the second loader fence's 'failure arms as above' is unpinned against a trim of the first fence's arms
fix_decisions.evidence: low-yield relative to cost: the phrase is prose-referential, and the existing loader-call and placement pins already bind the fence's presence and position

sweep_evidence.note: class-sweep over the changed surface: swept every diff-added numeric/ceiling claim in docs/review-and-fix-budget.md and docs/cutovers/issue-620-*.md against fresh python3 measurements (all other cells true); swept both universal-claim sites for the 'every fix loop' class; swept all three f2162d SHA mirror sites

skipped_checks.reason: working tree dirty (this check grades committed HEAD) — re-run post-commit for a zero-skip result
